extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won a chaotic sprint in Cattolica after suffering a dropped chain, passing VIVIANI Elia and NIZZOLO Giacomo in the final meters.
tactical_patterns: MARENGO Umberto and TAGLIANI Filippo broke early when no other teams wanted energy drain on flat stages before climbs.
ALPECIN-FENIX led out MERLIER Tim but positioning right-side allowed EWAN Caleb to circumvent organized train despite mechanical.
NIZZOLO Giacomo sprinted isolated into wind on opposite road side, nearly held on—suggests team lead-out failure critical.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: recovered from dropped chain to win, shows exceptional bike handling and power reserve in chaotic finale
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: 11th second place at Giro reveals pattern of strong positioning but insufficient final acceleration to win
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: lost stage despite organized ALPECIN lead-out, suggests vulnerability when train breaks down in final 200m
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: abandoned race in ambulance, removes Bahrain leadership option and elevates CARUSO Damiano importance
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: negative: crashed with nervousness at 5km causing 13-minute loss, confirms GC ambitions over before mountains
rider_signals.DOMBROWSKI Joe: negative: crash inside 5km suggests positioning weakness on flat sprint stages, potential GC threat diminished
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: strong sprint for third but positioned too early, led out EWAN inadvertently by blocking wind
